title: GitHub Copilot Configuration
description: Generate repository-wide custom instructions, path-specific custom instructions, agent skills, prompt files, custom agents, and repository settings for GitHub Copilot.

steps.title: Repository-Wide Custom Instructions
steps.description: Configure .github/copilot-instructions.md — applied automatically to supported Copilot surfaces in this repository. Note: organization-level instructions exist but are outside the scope of this generator.
steps.hint: Repository-wide instructions are best used for stable project context: coding standards, stack details, build/test commands, and durable conventions. Avoid editor-specific UI shortcuts or transient guidance in this file.

fields.label: File Heading
fields.description: The top-level heading written at the top of the file.

fields.label: Coding Standards & Conventions
fields.description: Stable style rules, naming conventions, and patterns Copilot should consistently follow.
fields.screen_hint: These durable principles are always included. Add stack-specific conventions on top. Click presets to add common developer standards.
fields.locked_value:
- Validate all external input at system boundaries. Never trust data from users, APIs, or files without validation.
- Never hardcode credentials, API keys, tokens, or secrets. Use environment variables or secure vaults.
- Handle all errors explicitly. Never silently swallow exceptions or return undefined.
- Write descriptive, intention-revealing names for variables, functions, classes, and types.
- Use type annotations wherever they improve clarity and catch errors early.
- Keep functions small and focused. Each should do one thing well.
- Prefer existing project patterns over introducing new abstractions. Consistency beats novelty.
- Review every suggestion critically; do not accept something you cannot explain.
- Always run tests before committing. Never skip the test suite.

presets.label: TypeScript strict mode
presets.value: - Use TypeScript strict mode. Prefer explicit types at all public API boundaries.

presets.label: No `any` types
presets.description: Use `unknown` and narrow with type guards
presets.value: - Never use `any` — use `unknown` and narrow with type guards.

presets.label: Named exports only
presets.description: Avoid default exports for better refactoring
presets.value: - Use named exports exclusively. Avoid default exports.

presets.label: Async/await only
presets.value: - Use async/await throughout. Never use .then() chains or callbacks.

presets.label: Immutability first
presets.value: - Prefer immutability. Use const and readonly; avoid in-place mutation where possible.

presets.label: Error handling
presets.value: - Handle errors explicitly. Never silently swallow exceptions.

presets.label: Small focused changes
presets.description: One concern per PR or commit
presets.value: - Keep changes small and focused — one concern per PR.

presets.label: No magic numbers
presets.value: - Extract magic numbers and strings into named constants.

fields.label: Install command
fields.description: Command to install project dependencies.

fields.label: Dev command
fields.description: Command to start the development environment.

fields.label: Build command
fields.description: Command to build the project.

fields.label: Test command
fields.description: Command to run the project test suite.

fields.label: Lint command
fields.description: Command to run static linting.

fields.label: Typecheck command
fields.description: Command to run type checking.

fields.label: CI command
fields.description: Command that mirrors the project's CI pipeline.

fields.label: Architecture & Stack Notes
fields.description: Key architecture decisions, stack choices, and design patterns Copilot should respect.
fields.placeholder:
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
- Backend: Python 3.13 + FastAPI + Pydantic v2
- State management: React hooks (no Redux or Zustand)
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4
fields.screen_hint: Describe your stack so Copilot recommends patterns that fit your architecture. Click a preset to load a common stack description.

presets.label: React + Vite + TypeScript
presets.value:
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4
- State: React hooks — no Redux or Zustand

presets.label: FastAPI + Pydantic
presets.value:
- Backend: Python 3.13 + FastAPI + Pydantic v2
- ORM: SQLAlchemy (async)
- Auth: JWT tokens

presets.label: Next.js full-stack
presets.value:
- Framework: Next.js 15 App Router
- Language: TypeScript strict mode
- Styling: Tailwind CSS
- DB: Prisma + PostgreSQL

fields.label: Security Baseline
fields.description: Stable security expectations that apply across all repository interactions.
fields.locked_value:
- Never commit secrets, API keys, tokens, or credentials to source control.
- Validate all external inputs before use.
- Follow the principle of least privilege for access and permissions.
- Prefer safe defaults over convenience in security-sensitive code.

presets.label: Dependency security
presets.value:
- Prefer well-maintained libraries with active security patching.
- Pin dependency versions for reproducible builds.
- Check for known vulnerabilities before adding any package.

presets.label: Sensitive data handling
presets.value:
- Never log secrets or PII.
- Mask or redact sensitive values in telemetry and logs.
- Use encrypted storage for credentials and keys.

fields.label: Protected Paths
fields.description: Repository paths and files that should generally not be modified by automated tools.
fields.placeholder: - Do not modify generated code or vendor directories.
fields.locked_value:
- Do not modify generated, vendored, or third-party files.
- Do not edit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Pipfile.lock, go.sum) by hand.
- Treat deployment manifests and binary artifacts as protected unless the task explicitly requires it.

presets.label: Common generated files
presets.value:
- Do not modify files under dist/, build/, or .next/.
- Do not edit package-lock.json or yarn.lock directly.
- Do not modify __pycache__/ or *.pyc files.

fields.label: Dependency & Package Policy
fields.description: Rules for adding, updating, or removing dependencies.
fields.placeholder:
- Prefer stable, well-maintained packages.
- Avoid unnecessary new dependencies.

presets.label: Conservative dependency additions
presets.value:
- Do not introduce new dependencies without explicit approval.
- Prefer built-in libraries and existing dependencies over new ones.

fields.label: Stable Workflow Guidance
fields.description: General workflow expectations that are durable and apply across Copilot surfaces.
fields.placeholder:
- Review suggestions critically.
- Keep code changes small and incremental.
- Validate security-sensitive code and tests before committing.
fields.default:
- Review suggestions critically and refuse anything that does not match project conventions.
- Keep changes small and incremental; prefer focused pull requests.
- Use tests to verify behavior before accepting changes.
- Validate security-sensitive code carefully, including auth, input validation, and secrets handling.
fields.screen_hint: This field is for stable workflow guidance, not editor-specific shortcuts or transient UI commands. Use it to define engineering habits that apply across surfaces.

steps.title: Path-Specific Instruction Files
steps.description: Create one or more .instructions.md files under .github/instructions/ for path-based guidance. Multiple files may apply and can combine with repo-wide instructions; conflicts are warned.
steps.hint: Use one file per concern and target via the applyTo frontmatter. The generator will warn on conflicting semantics between path-specific and repo-wide instructions.

fields.label: Instruction files
fields.description: Define individual .instructions.md files. Each entry becomes a separate file under .github/instructions/

fields.label: File name
fields.description: Filename relative to .github/instructions/, e.g. testing.instructions.md

fields.label: applyTo (glob)
fields.description: Glob pattern(s) to match files this instruction applies to

fields.label: Instruction Body
fields.description: Markdown body for this instruction file
fields.screen_hint: Keep each file focused; multiple matching files are combined at runtime and may produce ambiguous results if they conflict.

steps.title: Agent Skills
steps.description: Define reusable Copilot skills. Each skill should live in its own directory under .github/skills/ and include a SKILL.md with required frontmatter (name, description).
steps.hint: Model each skill as a directory with a SKILL.md file; this structured format helps validation and prevents invalid skill blobs.

fields.label: Skills
fields.description: One entry per skill directory. Each entry produces a <directory>/SKILL.md file under .github/skills/

fields.label: Directory name
fields.description: Directory under .github/skills/, e.g. debug-ci

fields.label: Skill name
fields.description: Frontmatter 'name' for SKILL.md

fields.label: Skill description
fields.description: Frontmatter 'description' for SKILL.md

fields.label: SKILL.md Body
fields.description: Body content for SKILL.md (after frontmatter)

steps.title: Prompt Files
steps.description: Create .github/prompts/*.prompt.md files with frontmatter and body. Prompt files are currently in public preview and supported only in VS Code, Visual Studio, and JetBrains IDEs.
steps.hint: Prompt files are in public preview — generated prompts may not be available across all Copilot surfaces. This step will emit one prompt file per entry.

fields.label: Prompt files
fields.description: One prompt file per entry. Each becomes .github/prompts/<filename>

fields.label: File name
fields.description: Filename under .github/prompts/, e.g. release-note.prompt.md

fields.label: Description
fields.description: Frontmatter description for the prompt file

fields.label: Agent (optional)
fields.description: Optional agent this prompt targets (if applicable)

fields.label: Prompt Body
fields.description: Prompt content (Markdown allowed)

steps.title: Agent Instruction Files
steps.description: Configure AGENTS.md guidance for agentic workflows. The generator can emit a root AGENTS.md plus optional nested AGENTS.md files; the root file is treated as the primary instruction source and nested files are additional guidance rather than strict overrides.
steps.hint: AGENTS.md is specifically relevant to agentic scenarios. Use nested AGENTS.md for subtree-specific additional guidance; the root AGENTS.md is considered primary.

fields.label: Generate AGENTS.md instruction files
fields.description: Enable root and optional subtree AGENTS.md guidance files when custom agents or agentic workflows are required.

fields.label: AGENTS.md Content
fields.description: Write root AGENTS.md content and optionally describe nested subtree additional guidance for AGENTS.md files.
fields.placeholder:
# Agent instructions
- Use the security reviewer for security-related changes.
- Use the test writer for new or changed code that needs coverage.
fields.screen_hint: AGENTS.md is an instruction file, not an agent profile. Keep it concise and focused on agent behavior expectations. If you need reusable agents, use the Custom Agents step instead.

steps.title: Custom Agents
steps.description: Define custom Copilot agents with specific roles, tool access, optional MCP server config, and scope.
steps.hint: Custom agents are specialist profiles. Use repository-scoped agents for shared team workflows, or user-scoped agents for personal specialist profiles. The renderer will choose output paths based on agent scope.

fields.label: Agent Scope
fields.description: Choose whether these agents are shared in the repository or intended for personal user scope.

fields.label: Copilot Agents
fields.description: Define one or more custom agents. Each becomes a separate agent profile file.
fields.screen_hint: Each agent gets a system prompt that defines its role and boundary. Restrict tools to only what the agent needs — narrower permissions make agents safer, more reliable, and faster. The renderer will emit repository agents under .github/agents/ and user agents under ~/.copilot/agents/ depending on scope.

presets.label: Security Reviewer
presets.description: Reviews code changes for security issues

value.name: security-reviewer
value.description: Reviews code changes for security vulnerabilities and suggests actionable fixes
value.system_prompt:
Review the provided code changes for security vulnerabilities including injection flaws, broken authentication, sensitive data exposure, missing authorisation checks, and insecure dependencies.

For each issue: state the severity, describe the problem, and provide a concrete fix. Focus only on security — not style or architecture.

presets.label: PR Reviewer
presets.description: Reviews pull requests for correctness and maintainability

value.name: pr-reviewer
value.description: Reviews pull requests for correctness, consistency, and maintainability
value.system_prompt: Perform a thorough code review of the provided changes as a senior engineer. Cover: correctness, logic errors, edge cases, consistency with existing patterns, missing error handling, and performance red flags. Be constructive — for each issue explain why it matters and how to fix it.

presets.label: Test Writer
presets.description: Writes tests for new or changed code

value.name: test-writer
value.description: Writes unit and integration tests for new or changed code using the project's existing test framework
value.system_prompt: Write tests for the code you are given. Use the testing framework already present in the project. Cover happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions. Keep each test focused on one behaviour. Run the tests after writing them and fix any failures.

presets.label: Documentation Writer
presets.description: Writes inline docs and README content

value.name: documentation-writer
value.description: Writes and updates inline documentation and README content following project conventions
value.system_prompt: Write and update documentation for the provided code. Match the documentation style already used in the project. Document all public APIs with a summary, parameters, return values, and usage examples where helpful. Do not modify source logic.

steps.title: Repository Settings (.github/copilot/settings.json)
steps.description: Configure repository-level Copilot settings committed for your whole team. Note: many repository keys are ignored by Copilot; unsupported keys will be written but may have no effect.
steps.hint: Repository settings are limited. The generator emits selected keys; unsupported or future keys should be provided via the optional field marked for unsupported settings.

fields.label: Company Announcements
fields.description: A short announcement shown in all Copilot contexts for this repo.
fields.default: Always run tests before committing. Review suggestions critically and validate changes against project conventions.

fields.label: Unsupported or Future Settings (JSON)
fields.description: Optional JSON blob for unsupported or future repository-level settings. These keys are often ignored by Copilot and may have no effect; use with caution.
fields.screen_hint: Unsupported keys in repository settings are typically ignored by Copilot. Prefer hooks or other supported mechanisms for enforcement.

steps.title: Repository Hooks (.github/hooks/)
steps.description: Optional repository hooks configuration emitted under .github/hooks/. Use hooks for enforcement-style behavior instead of embedding unsupported keys in settings.json.

fields.label: Hook files
fields.description: Define hook JSON files to emit under .github/hooks/
